topic: Write and Edit tools -- examples and best practices in AI agent harnesses

sources.title: Claude Code Tools Reference
keyInsights: Lists Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit as distinct built-in tools with permission-required gating
Edit makes targeted edits to specific files; Write creates or overwrites; NotebookEdit modifies Jupyter notebook cells
LSP tool auto-reports type errors and warnings after edits, enabling self-correction loops

sources.title: Anthropic Text Editor Tool (str_replace_based_edit_tool)
keyInsights: Schema-less Anthropic-provided tool with commands view, str_replace, create, insert
text_editor_20250728 adds optional max_characters parameter for truncating file views
Claude 4 line removed the undo_edit command; earlier 20250124 version had it
Standard error messages include 'Found N matches', 'No match found', and 'File not found' with is_error: true
Line numbers in view output are essential for insert_line and view_range parameters

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK Overview
keyInsights: Agent SDK exposes Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Monitor, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch, AskUserQuestion as built-ins
allowed_tools / allowedTools narrows capability per agent
PostToolUse hooks with matcher Edit|Write enable audit-logging every file mutation

sources.title: Claude Code Best Practices
keyInsights: Verification (tests, screenshots, expected outputs) is the single highest-leverage practice
Explore-plan-code-commit workflow separates research from execution
CLAUDE.md should be concise; bloated memory files cause Claude to ignore instructions
Common failure pattern: correcting over and over pollutes context with failed approaches

sources.title: Claude Code Common Workflows
keyInsights: Plan Mode uses read-only operations so exploration doesn't mutate files
@-mentions add files to context without waiting for Claude to Read them
Worktree isolation lets parallel sessions edit without collision

sources.title: Aider Edit Formats Explained
keyInsights: Four formats: whole, diff (SEARCH/REPLACE), diff-fenced, udiff
diff-fenced is a Gemini-specific workaround for fencing issues
udiff was specifically built to reduce lazy-coding placeholders

sources.title: Aider Unified Diffs
keyInsights: udiff improved benchmark accuracy from 20% to 61% on GPT-4 Turbo
Cut laziness 3X by psychologically cueing the model it's writing for a machine
Design principles: FAMILIAR, SIMPLE, HIGH-LEVEL, FLEXIBLE
Aider's parser applies nine recovery strategies before giving up

sources.title: Aider Polyglot Leaderboard
keyInsights: GPT-5 (high) + diff: 88.0% correct on polyglot benchmark
o3-pro (high) + diff: 84.9%
Gemini 2.5 Pro + diff-fenced: 83.1% with 99.6% well-formed responses
o3 + gpt-4.1 architect mode: 78.2% with 100% well-formed

sources.title: Aider's Original Edit Format Benchmark (July 2023)
keyInsights: whole format scored 46% on GPT-3.5 first-attempt, beating all function-call variants
GPT-3.5 frequently returned invalid function-call JSON with name: python
Complex edit formats divert model focus from coding

sources.title: Aider January 2024 GPT-4 Turbo Benchmarks
keyInsights: GPT-4 Turbo 0125 performed worse with udiff than November 2023 predecessor
Same model improved on SEARCH/REPLACE format vs previous release
udiff remained best-performing format overall despite regression

sources.title: Aider on Claude 3 and Edit Formats
keyInsights: Claude 3 Opus worked best with SEARCH/REPLACE blocks
Claude 3 Sonnet only reliable on whole-file format
Edit-format capability correlates directly with model capability

sources.title: Aider Troubleshooting: Edit Errors
keyInsights: Over 25k tokens of context degrades instruction-following
Weaker models disobey system prompts about edit format
Architect mode uses a separate editor model for reliability

sources.title: Aider: Best LLMs and Edit Format Pairings
keyInsights: Top models for Aider: Gemini 2.5 Pro, DeepSeek R1/V3, Claude 3.7 Sonnet, o3/o4-mini, GPT-4.1
Sub-GPT-3.5 models typically cannot reliably use diff formats

sources.title: Aider's SEARCH/REPLACE Prompt Source
keyInsights: SEARCH section must EXACTLY MATCH file content character-for-character
Only first match is replaced per block
Break large changes into multiple small blocks
Use two blocks (delete + insert) to move code

sources.title: Aider's Unified Diff Prompt Source
keyInsights: Generate edits matching diff -U0 output
Omit line numbers from @@ headers
Replace entire function blocks when modifying functions
Preserve indentation precisely

sources.title: Aider SEARCH/REPLACE Parser Source
keyInsights: Fuzzy matching with ~80% similarity threshold, ±10% length variation
Whitespace normalization detects uniform indentation drift
Ellipsis (...) expansion splits blocks at placeholders
Cross-file matching when a single-file match fails

sources.title: OpenAI apply_patch Tool Documentation
keyInsights: Three operations: create_file, update_file, delete_file
Agents SDK provides applyDiff and ApplyPatchTool helpers
Path validation is harness responsibility; prevent directory traversal
Pair with shell tool for file discovery
Decide rollback behavior explicitly for multi-patch operations

sources.title: OpenAI GPT-4.1 Prompting Guide (V4A format)
keyInsights: V4A wraps patches in *** Begin Patch / *** End Patch sentinels
Uses 3 lines of context before and after each change
@@ anchors scope location to classes/functions
No line numbers; context lines identify locations
SEARCH/REPLACE and pseudo-XML formats also tested well

sources.title: OpenAI Codex apply-patch Source
keyInsights: Reference parser tolerates relative and absolute paths
Fuzzy matching handles Unicode normalization (curly quotes vs ASCII)
Multi-chunk files use repeated @@ sections
Replacements apply in descending order to preserve indices

sources.title: Cline All Tools Reference
keyInsights: write_to_file (path, content) creates or overwrites files
replace_in_file performs targeted SEARCH/REPLACE-style edits
Tool protocol uses XML tags with nested path and content
Heuristic: write_to_file for new files or >50% rewrites, replace_in_file otherwise

sources.title: Cline Background Edit Feature
keyInsights: Streams line-by-line diff to chat panel instead of opening diff editor
Pairs well with auto-approve mode for bulk small edits
Checkpoints still created for rollback

sources.title: OpenCode Edit Tool Description
keyInsights: Read-before-Edit invariant enforced (must Read or operation fails)
Match indentation precisely; never include line-number prefix
Multi-match requires context or replaceAll flag
Explicit guidance: avoid emojis unless user requests them
